text:
I'll search for all papers simultaneously to check their existence.
The VGGT search returned no results, so I need to do one more search for it.
Both papers are clearly real and well-documented. Here is the final verdict:

---

**ITEM 0 — Tversky (2003), "Structures of mental spaces: How people think about space"**


Tversky, B. (2003). "Structures of mental spaces: How people think about space." *Environment and Behavior*, 35, 66–80.
 This paper is confirmed across multiple academic sources including SAGE Journals, PsycNET, and Springer. The author, venue, and year all match exactly.

**ITEM 1 — Wang et al. (2025), "VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer…"**


The paper is confirmed in the CVPR 2025 Open Access repository, authored by Wang, Jianyuan; Chen, Minghao; Karaev, Nikita; Vedaldi, Andrea; Rupprecht, Christian; and Novotny, David.
 
It is also a **CVPR 2025 Best Paper Award** recipient.
 The cited title adds the phrase "for universal 3d reconstruction," which is a minor embellishment not in the official title, but the core paper is entirely real.

---

```json
  {
    "index": 0,
    "verdict": "UNLIKELY",
    "explanation": "The paper is confirmed real across multiple academic databases (SAGE Journals, PsycNET, Springer, Academia.edu). Author Barbara Tversky, venue 'Environment and Behavior', and year 2003 all match exactly. No hallucination indicators present."
  },
  {
    "index": 1,
    "verdict": "UNLIKELY",
    "explanation": "The paper is confirmed real. It is published at CVPR 2025 (Open Access), authored by Jianyuan Wang et al., available on arXiv (2503.11651), and was awarded the CVPR 2025 Best Paper Award. The only discrepancy is the cited title appends 'for universal 3d reconstruction', which is a minor embellishment not in the official title ('VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer'), but the paper itself is unambiguously real."
  }
]
```
